entries.name: Cleland Wildlife Park
entries.desc: A free-roaming wildlife park in the Adelaide Hills where kangaroos wander through the grounds and visitors can hand-feed them, hold koalas, and walk among emus and wallabies in a naturalistic bush setting.

photo.alt: Kangaroos resting on the grass at Cleland Wildlife Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: cleland-wildlife-park-kangaroos.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Kangaroo Land in Cleland Wildlife Park" by Cdzhangyin · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Kangaroo_Land_in_Cleland_Wildlife_Park.jpg -->

entries.name: Monkey Mia Dolphin Interaction
entries.desc: A UNESCO World Heritage site where a small pod of wild bottlenose dolphins has been wading into the shallows to interact with people for decades. Rangers supervise brief, managed morning encounters at the beach.

photo.alt: Visitors wading in the shallows as a wild dolphin approaches at Monkey Mia
photo.credit: <!-- photo: monkey-mia-dolphin-encounter.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Monkey Mia, Western Australia" by Nachoman-au · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Monkey_Mia,_Western_Australia.jpg -->

entries.name: Ningaloo Reef Whale Shark Swim
entries.desc: The world's most reliable place to swim with whale sharks in the wild, operating during the annual March–July season when these giant filter feeders aggregate in the warm waters off the Ningaloo coast.

photo.alt: Whale shark swimming in the waters off Ningaloo Reef
photo.credit: <!-- photo: ningaloo-reef-whale-shark.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Requin baleine ningaloo reef" by Chewb62 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Requin_baleine_ningaloo_reef.JPG -->

entries.name: Shark Ray Alley
entries.tag: Hol Chan Marine Reserve, Ambergris Caye
entries.desc: A shallow sandbar inside the Hol Chan Marine Reserve where nurse sharks and southern stingrays congregate in large numbers, having been conditioned over decades by fishing boat activity. Snorkelers swim directly among them.

photo.alt: Nurse shark swimming over the reef near Ambergris Caye, Belize
photo.credit: <!-- photo: belize-barrier-reef-nurse-shark.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public domain · "Nurse Shark 4474" by Jst525 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Nurse_Shark_4474.jpg -->

entries.name: Toucan Rescue Ranch
entries.desc: A wildlife rehabilitation center near San José that rescues and rehabilitates sloths, toucans, owls, and other native Costa Rican wildlife. Guided tours bring visitors close to animals at various stages of recovery before release.

photo.alt: Keel-billed toucan perched on a branch in Costa Rica
photo.credit: <!-- photo: keel-billed-toucan-costa-rica.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Keel billed toucan costa rica" by Ttschleuder · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Keel_billed_toucan_costa_rica.jpg -->

entries.name: Galápagos Islands Wildlife Encounters
entries.desc: The Galápagos are unmatched for fearless wildlife. Sea lions sleep on benches, marine iguanas sun themselves on footpaths, blue-footed boobies nest within arm's reach, and giant tortoises lumber through highland pastures — animals that evolved without natural predators and remain completely unafraid of people.

photo.alt: Galápagos sea lion on volcanic rocks at San Cristóbal
photo.credit: <!-- photo: galapagos-sea-lion.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Galápagos sea lion San Cristóbal Galápagos Ecuador DSC00189 ad" by Adavyd · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Gal%C3%A1pagos_sea_lion_San_Crist%C3%B3bal_Gal%C3%A1pagos_Ecuador_DSC00189_ad.JPG -->

entries.name: Nara Deer Park
entries.desc: Over 1,200 wild sika deer roam freely through the park grounds and surrounding streets of central Nara, considered sacred messengers of the gods. They approach visitors, bow for deer crackers sold at kiosks, and wander among the temples.

photo.alt: Sika deer resting on the lawns of Nara Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: nara-park-sika-deer.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.1 jp · "Sika Deer, Nara Park 01" by asanopiyo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Sika_Deer,_Nara_Park_01.jpg -->

entries.name: Ōkunoshima (Rabbit Island)
entries.desc: A small island in the Seto Inland Sea overrun by hundreds of feral domestic rabbits that swarm visitors in search of food. Reachable by a short ferry, the island offers an unusual and extremely tactile experience with animals that have no fear of people.

photo.alt: Feral rabbits being hand-fed on Ōkunoshima
photo.credit: <!-- photo: okunoshima-rabbits.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Okunojima-rabbit" by NY066 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Okunojima-rabbit.JPG -->

entries.name: Giraffe Centre
entries.desc: A breeding center for the endangered Rothschild's giraffe on the outskirts of Nairobi where visitors stand on an elevated platform and hand-feed adult giraffes, who often press their long tongues into your palm. Run by the African Fund for Endangered Wildlife.

photo.alt: Rothschild's giraffe feeding from an elevated platform, tongue extended
photo.credit: <!-- photo: giraffe-centre-feeding.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.5 · "Giraffe feeding.jpg" by Hdahlmo~commonswiki · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Giraffe_feeding.jpg -->

entries.name: David Sheldrick Wildlife Trust
entries.desc: The world's most successful elephant orphan rescue and rehabilitation program, operating within Nairobi National Park. A daily public visit at 11 a.m. lets a small group watch orphaned baby elephants mud-bath, play, and interact with their keepers before returning to the bush.

photo.alt: Orphaned elephants and keepers at a watering hole in Nairobi National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sheldrick-elephant-orphans-watering-hole.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Elephant and keepers near watering hole at Sheldrick Centre, Nairobi.jpg" by Daniel Case · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Elephant_and_keepers_near_watering_hole_at_Sheldrick_Centre,_Nairobi.jpg -->

entries.name: Sepilok Orangutan Rehabilitation Centre
entries.desc: One of only four orangutan sanctuaries in the world, set in a protected forest reserve outside Sandakan. Twice-daily feedings at an open-air platform bring wild and semi-wild orangutans down from the canopy, offering close and unscripted encounters.

photo.alt: Bornean orangutan at Sepilok Rehabilitation Centre
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sepilok-orangutan.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Bornean Orangutan (Pongo pygmaeus) (14585882925).jpg" by Bernard DUPONT · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bornean_Orangutan_(Pongo_pygmaeus)_(14585882925).jpg -->

entries.name: Hanifaru Bay Manta Ray Aggregation
entries.desc: A UNESCO Biosphere Reserve and one of the world's most important manta ray feeding grounds. During the southwest monsoon season (June–November), hundreds of reef and oceanic manta rays gather to feed on plankton-rich upwellings in a single bay, snorkelers permitted.

photo.alt: Reef manta rays feeding inside Hanifaru Bay, Maldives
photo.credit: <!-- photo: hanifaru-bay-manta-ray.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 4.0 · "Reef Manta in the Maldives 09347.jpg" by Ben Shoshana · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Reef_Manta_in_the_Maldives_09347.jpg -->

entries.name: Isla Mujeres Whale Shark Tour
entries.tag: Isla Holbox / Isla Mujeres, Quintana Roo
entries.desc: Every summer (June–September), the largest aggregation of whale sharks on Earth gathers in the warm waters off the Yucatán Peninsula. Regulated tours by boat bring small groups into the water to snorkel alongside dozens of these gentle filter feeders, some over 12 meters long.

photo.alt: Snorkeler swimming alongside a whale shark off Isla Holbox
photo.credit: <!-- photo: whale-shark-holbox.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 2.0 · "Whale shark Holbox island Mexico Walhai (20184988951).jpg" by dronepicr · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Whale_shark_Holbox_island_Mexico_Walhai_(20184988951).jpg -->

entries.name: Gray Whale Encounter, Laguna San Ignacio
entries.tag: San Ignacio Lagoon, Baja California Sur
entries.desc: The lagoons of Baja California are the only place on Earth where wild gray whales actively seek out small boats and allow themselves to be touched. Mothers bring their calves alongside pangas and lift them to be petted — the most extraordinary voluntary human-whale interaction in the world.

photo.alt: Adult gray whale and calf approaching a boat in Baja California
photo.credit: <!-- photo: gray-whale-calf-baja.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 3.0 · "Ballena gris adulta con su ballenato.jpg" by José Eugenio Gómez Rodríguez · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Ballena_gris_adulta_con_su_ballenato.jpg -->

entries.name: Kaikōura Sperm Whale Watching
entries.desc: A deep submarine canyon close to shore makes Kaikōura one of the only places in the world where sperm whales can be reliably seen year-round from small boats. The town is also a hub for swimming with dusky dolphins and seeing New Zealand fur seals.

photo.alt: Sperm whale diving off the Kaikōura coast
photo.credit: <!-- photo: sperm-whale-kaikoura.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Destination Kaikoura-513967-whale-tail.jpg" by Whale Watch Kaikoura / Destination Kaikōura · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Destination_Kaikoura-513967-whale-tail.jpg -->

entries.name: Philippine Tarsier Sanctuary
entries.desc: One of the few sanctuaries where the Philippine tarsier — one of the world's smallest primates, with enormous eyes and the ability to rotate its head 180 degrees — can be seen in a protected semi-wild setting. Strict silence protocols protect these highly sensitive animals.

photo.alt: Philippine tarsier at the sanctuary in Corella, Bohol
photo.credit: <!-- photo: philippine-tarsier-corella.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "IMG 0585 Philippine Tarsier.jpg" by Jeroen Hellingman · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:IMG_0585_Philippine_Tarsier.jpg -->

entries.name: Boulders Beach African Penguin Colony
entries.desc: A sheltered beach within Table Mountain National Park that is home to a colony of over 2,000 African penguins. Boardwalks bring visitors within meters of nesting birds, and penguins routinely wander the town streets and adjacent swimming beach.

photo.alt: African penguins on the boardwalk at Boulders Beach
photo.credit: <!-- photo: boulders-beach-african-penguins.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "African penguin near Boulders Beach.jpg" by Paul Mannix · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:African_penguin_near_Boulders_Beach.jpg -->

entries.name: Elephant Nature Park
entries.desc: A rescue and rehabilitation sanctuary for elephants in northern Thailand's Mae Taeng valley. Visitors walk among freely roaming rescued elephants, feed them, and observe their social behavior in a forested sanctuary where the animals are never ridden or made to perform.

photo.alt: Rescued Asian elephants at Elephant Nature Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: elephant-nature-park-calves.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY 4.0 · "Elephanteaux d'Asie (Elephant nature park, Thaïlande).jpg" by Christelle EL JAMALI · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Elephanteaux_d%27Asie_(Elephant_nature_park,_Tha%C3%AFlande).jpg -->

entries.name: Mountain Gorilla Trekking, Bwindi
entries.desc: One of the few places on Earth where tourists can observe wild mountain gorillas at close range. Guided treks through dense forest lead to habituated family groups, where a one-hour visit allows face-to-face encounters with silverbacks, mothers, and juveniles. Uganda offers more gorilla families for trekking than anywhere else.

photo.alt: Mountain gorilla in Bwindi Impenetrable National Park
photo.credit: <!-- photo: bwindi-mountain-gorilla.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 2.0 · "Mountain Gorilla, Bwindi, Uganda (16372469599).jpg" by Rod Waddington · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Mountain_Gorilla,_Bwindi,_Uganda_(16372469599).jpg -->

entries.name: Crystal River Manatee Swim
entries.desc: The only place in the United States where it is legal to swim with wild manatees. Every winter, hundreds of West Indian manatees gather in the warm spring-fed waters of Kings Bay to escape the cold Gulf of Mexico. Snorkeling tours operate from November through March.

photo.alt: Florida manatee in Crystal River's spring-fed waters
photo.credit: <!-- photo: crystal-river-florida-manatee.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Florida Manatee FWS 21.jpg" by Keith Ramos / USFWS · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Florida_Manatee_FWS_21.jpg -->

entries.name: Katmai Brown Bear Viewing
entries.tag: Brooks Falls, Katmai National Park, Alaska
entries.desc: Every July, dozens of brown bears gather at Brooks Falls to catch sockeye salmon leaping upstream. Viewing platforms elevated above the falls bring visitors within meters of bears at peak fishing intensity — one of the most concentrated and accessible large-predator experiences in the world.

photo.alt: Brown bears fishing for salmon at Brooks Falls
photo.credit: <!-- photo: katmai-brooks-falls-brown-bears.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC BY-SA 3.0 · "Brown bears brooks falls.jpg" by Mbz1 · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Brown_bears_brooks_falls.jpg -->

entries.name: Assateague Island Wild Horses
entries.desc: A barrier island straddling Maryland and Virginia where feral horses have lived for centuries, likely descended from livestock shipwrecks. The horses wander campgrounds, beaches, and roadsides, sometimes approaching cars, and are managed separately by the two states — no fencing separates the animals from visitors.

photo.alt: Wild horses on the beach at Assateague Island
photo.credit: <!-- photo: assateague-island-wild-horses.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Assateague's wild horses are well known, even to many people who have never been to the island. The "wild" horses on Assateague (bd92b5c2-1dd8-b71b-0b0f-e040c7f50fff).JPG" by NPS Photo · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Assateague%27s_wild_horses_are_well_known,_even_to_many_people_who_have_never_been_to_the_island._The_%22wild%22_horses_on_Assateague_(bd92b5c2-1dd8-b71b-0b0f-e040c7f50fff).JPG -->

entries.name: Pacific Whale Watching, San Juan Islands
entries.desc: The inland waters around the San Juan Islands are home to resident and transient orca pods, with a viewing season running spring through fall. Trips from Friday Harbor and Anacortes put passengers alongside orcas, minke whales, and Dall's porpoises in the cold clear waters of Puget Sound.

photo.alt: Orca surfacing near the San Juan Islands
photo.credit: <!-- photo: san-juan-islands-orca.jpg — Wikimedia Commons CC0 · "Male orca whale L79 southern resident puget sound.jpg" by Erik Stockdale · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Male_orca_whale_L79_southern_resident_puget_sound.jpg -->

entries.name: Yellowstone Bison and Wolf Watching
entries.tag: Lamar Valley, Yellowstone National Park, Wyoming
entries.desc: Lamar Valley is often called "the Serengeti of North America." Bison herds of hundreds move through the valley, and the reintroduced wolf packs are watched daily by enthusiasts with spotting scopes. Grizzly bears, pronghorn, and elk round out a wildlife density unmatched in the lower 48 states.

photo.alt: Bison herd in Yellowstone's Lamar Valley
photo.credit: <!-- photo: yellowstone-lamar-valley-bison.jpg — Wikimedia Commons Public Domain · "Bison grazing at sunset in Lamar Valley during InstaMeet (34956051553).jpg" by Yellowstone National Park · https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Bison_grazing_at_sunset_in_Lamar_Valley_during_InstaMeet_(34956051553).jpg -->
